title: Why can’t every country pay for disasters like Jamaica?
content: Countries don’t follow a single disaster‑finance model because real‑world constraints differ. Jamaica shows the classic layered approach, while Grenada, Kenya and Malawi adapt based on regulations, partners and fiscal incentives.

title: Kenya’s double climate crisis: it needs funds to adapt, and disaster aid is damaging the environment
content: Kenya faces worsening floods and droughts while lacking funds for climate adaptation. Humanitarian aid often harms the environment, and major financing gaps hinder long‑term solutions. Greener aid and better climate funding are urgently needed.

title: The ski industry is oddly quiet on climate change
content: The problem is a major threat to the U.S. snowsports industry. But their actions fall short.

title: A finite element investigation into the in-plane load bearing behaviour of traditional timber-dry stone (Kath-Kuni) masonry walls
content: This study presents a detailed finite element investigation into the in‑plane load‑bearing behaviour of traditional Kath‑Kuni timber–dry‑stone masonry walls, a construction typology widely used in the seismically active Himalayan region.

title: Engineering Structures
content: Engineering Structures provides a forum for a broad blend of scientific and technical papers to reflect the evolving needs of the structural engineering and structural mechanics communities.

title: Ignore test
content: This is a test.

title: From forecasts to foresight: Strengthening Mongolia’s climate intelligence through partnership
content: UNDP and Mongolia mark 50 years of partnership, highlighting major upgrades to NAMEM’s climate‑monitoring systems—including new high‑performance computing—to improve forecasting, early warning, and climate‑resilient national planning.

title: Vulnerability outpaces climate worry in U.S. frontline communities
content: Climate change affects everyone, but not equally. Communities facing the most exposure to climate impacts are often called frontline communities.

title: How water security helps rural communities in Asia adapt to climate change
content: To protect communities and livelihoods, governments are increasingly emphasizing water in National Adaptation Plans and Nationally Determined Contributions, and investing in climate-resilient water systems.

title: From planning to action: How Saint Lucia fast-tracked a new era of urban flood resilience
content: Saint Lucia has faced years of increasingly heavy rains, flash floods, and overflowing rivers, events that hit hardest in communities where homes, schools, and small businesses sit close to waterways.

title: International conference on crisis and disaster governance, planning and resilience: Integrating risk and security within international postgraduate education (ICPCDGR 2026)
content: The International Hybrid Conference on Crisis and Disaster Governance, Planning and Resilience (ICPCDGR 2026) is presented as a global platform designed to address the growing complexity of contemporary risks.

title: Why some regions are winning the fight against groundwater depletion
content: Study examines successful cases of aquifer recovery, highlighting how communities and resource managers can tackle groundwater depletion.

title: Why disaster risk financing must evolve to meet the climate crisis
content: Climate-related disasters are increasing, yet global A&R finance remains fragmented, reactive, and far below estimated needs. In a world increasingly shaped by climate extremes, the way we finance disaster risk is no longer fit for purpose.

title: Permafrost is key to carbon storage. That makes northern wildfires even more dangerous
content: Northern wildfires thaw permafrost and release long‑stored carbon, outweighing the cooling effect seen in Canada’s snow‑covered burn areas and increasing future climate‑warming risks.

title: Explainer: Citizen science
content: Citizen science blends community knowledge with digital tools to fill data gaps, improve environmental monitoring, and support better policymaking. It empowers local people, boosts inclusivity, and strengthens climate and water‑management decisions.

title: Honduras’ climate goals recognize conservation value of coastal wetlands
content: Honduras’ new climate goals commit to protecting and restoring mangroves and seagrass meadows, recognizing their role in storing carbon, safeguarding coasts, and supporting local communities. The plan sets measurable targets through 2035.

title: Africa’s urban moment: Building resilient cities for a changing world
content: Africa’s rapid urbanization brings major opportunities but also strains infrastructure and heightens climate risks. Cities must invest in climate‑smart systems, digital tools, inclusive services, and innovative financing to build long‑term resilience.

title: International Conference on GeoDRR–2026 Geography of Disaster in the 21st Century: Challenges, Preparedness and Adaptations (Hybrid Mode)
content: An international conference (GeoDRR 2026) will explore disaster geography, preparedness, and adaptation in the 21st century, bringing together global researchers and practitioners.

title: Tamil Nadu Open University
content: A State Open University established by the Tamil Nadu Government , Recognised by UGC-DEB, Accorded with 12(B) status by UGC Member in Asian Association of Open Universities and Association of Commonwealth Universities

title: When temperatures drop, Heart-related deaths rise
content: New research highlights an often overlooked cost of cold weather: months with lower temperatures see significantly greater rates of death from heart attacks, strokes, and coronary artery disease than milder months.

title: American college of cardiology (ACC)
content: The American College of Cardiology (ACC) envisions a world where science, knowledge and innovation optimize cardiovascular care and outcomes. It believes in the power of community.

title: Protecting homes from future flooding
content: Residents in Kensington’s Lorne Gardens will get flood defence measures installed in their homes, after the Council committed funding and secured contributions from Thames Water and Peabody.

title: The Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea (RBKC)
content: The Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea plays a central role in disaster risk reduction (DRR) at the local level by focusing on risk assessment and preparedness in coordination with wider London and national systems.

title: Solutions for better agri-risk management in the European Union
content: The global agri-food system is facing increasingly complex and interconnected risks.

title: Mauritania: Water brings dignity, hope and community resilience to Ndiorol
content: A new solar‑powered water system in Ndiorol, Mauritania brings reliable drinking water, boosts food security, empowers women, and strengthens community resilience through climate‑adaptation measures and local capacity building.

title: Multi-hazard resilient buildings and low-carbon solutions for a future-proof built environment
content: Simona Bianchi and Mauro Overend from Delft University of Technology explain why multi-disciplinary approaches are key to creating safer, eco-friendly, and resilient buildings that protect urban communities and ensure long-term sustainability.

title: What the coming El Niño climate pattern means for NZ in a warming world
content: El Niño is likely to return by NZ’s spring, amplifying global warming and bringing drier conditions to the north and east, wetter weather in the south and west, and potential economic impacts amid an already warming climate.

title: Learn, Connect, Share: Exploring resources for students in disaster resilience
content: Are you a student passionate about building a safer, more resilient world? Join us for a live introduction to PreventionWeb, UNDRR’s global platform for disaster risk reduction (DRR) knowledge.

title: Stronger together: Building post-disaster resilience through enhanced regional cooperation in the Indian Ocean
content: The purpose of this conversation is to illustrate the concrete solutions and approaches that have proven relevant in post-disaster settings, with particular emphasis on regional cooperation.

title: Unearthing uncertainty: Using risk models to make uncertainty visible
content: Catastrophe models play a critical role. It is understood that models are not predictors of precise outcomes; models offer frameworks for understanding and managing uncertainty.

title: What a gaping hole on a bridge reveals about aging infrastructure in Canada
content: When a large hole recently opened up in the deck of a bridge in Châteauguay, Québec, many people were understandably alarmed.

title: As Bolivia’s glaciers melt, new lakes threaten mountain communities
content: Melting glaciers in Bolivia are rapidly increasing the number of glacial lakes, raising the risk of dangerous glacial lake outburst floods (GLOFs) that threaten downstream communities.

title: From risk to resilience: how states are approaching insurance and climate risk in 2026
content: As rising climate risks drive insurance providers out of vulnerable markets, states are increasingly advancing policies that seek to transform insurance from a market of uncertainty and rising costs into a transparent tool for community resilience.

title: AI shows promise for flood forecasting and water security in data scarce regions
content: New research reveals that ‘foundation models’ trained on vast, general time‑series data may be able to forecast river flows accurately, even in regions with little or no local hydrological records.

title: When are wildfire evacuations most dangerous? Research finds leaving late risks lives
content: Research shows that delaying evacuation during wildfires increases risk, with driving conditions and social behavior making late evacuation particularly dangerous.

title: Unlocking nature for disaster resilience: a policy guide to enable nature-based solutions
content: This guide focuses on the role of NBS in climate resilience and disaster risk reduction (DRR), including traditional conservation and restoration practices when they contribute to resilience and human well-being.

title: Call for presenters: IRDR Young Scientists Lumos: “Economics of disasters and climate change: addressing inequalities, injustices, marginalisation and vulnerabilities”
content: This webinar explores how economic systems shape disaster risk, inequality, and climate impacts, inviting young researchers to present emerging insights.

title: Gender Observatory Report highlights progress and gaps in gender inclusion in disaster risk reduction
content: New report highlights progress and gaps in gender inclusion in global disaster risk discussions while showcasing how gender monitoring tools are being applied in national preparedness exercises.

title: State of the global climate 2025
content: This report outlines the WMO’s 2025 assessment of the global climate system, presenting updated data on greenhouse gases, global temperatures, ocean heat, sea‑level rise, acidification, glacier loss, sea‑ice decline and Earth’s energy imbalance.

title: Earth’s climate swings increasingly out of balance
content: Earth’s climate is increasingly out of balance, with record heat, rising seas, melting ice and extreme weather causing global disruption. WMO warns that every key climate indicator is worsening, demanding urgent action to protect people and ecosystems.
